message:
fix(printf): executor promotes args by format length modifier (bd-luc3d)

Added promote_args_by_format() that scans the format string and,
for each %[l|ll|j|z|t][diouxX] conversion, rewrites the
corresponding PrintfArg::Int -> PrintfArg::Long (or Ulong for
unsigned conversions). Called from execute_fprintf_case,
execute_snprintf_case, execute_sprintf_case before dispatch.

Removes from KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS: sprintf_t_d, sprintf_ll_d,
sprintf_j_d, sprintf_llx_max, sprintf_lld_negative,
sprintf_zd_negative, sprintf_l_d, sprintf_lo_max.

Long double (%Lf/%Le) remains skipped — f80 has no Rust
representation so we cannot preserve it across varargs from a
JSON f64 fixture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-0u5fm","title":"printf '*' negative width/precision: neg width should left-justify, neg precision should be ignored","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs, when width comes from '*' argument (Width::FromArg), a negative integer argument is not translated into '-' flag + abs(width) as C11 7.21.6.1 para 5 requires. Surfaced by bd-12hh printf_conformance matrix (sprintf_star_neg_width case: format='%*d', args=[-5, 42] — expected '42   ', got 2000+ spaces). resolve_width() also returns 0 for FromArg which means it never reads the * argument at all; the two bugs compound.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:21:55.984065866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:49:37.046999058Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Surfaced by bd-12hh printf_conformance matrix (sprintf_star_neg_width case: format='%*d', args=[-5, 42] — expected '42   ', got 2000+ spaces). resolve_width() also returns 0 for FromArg which means it never reads the * argument at all; the two bugs compound.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:21:55.984065866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:01:01.293518369Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:01:01.291039407Z","close_reason":"extract_va_args! now reads '*' width/precision as c_int (with sign-extension to u64), so render_printf's existing negative-value handling at lines 3139-3163 is reached. sprintf_star_neg_width and sprintf_star_neg_precision now pass end-to-end.  {"id":"bd-0udd","title":"Metamorphic proptests for SigSet bitset algebra","description":"frankenlibc_core::signal::SigSet is a 64-bit signal bitmask with add/del/is_member/empty/full operations. Unit tests cover point cases but no algebraic MRs fence the bitset semantics. Add: (1) add-then-is_member, (2) del-reverses-add, (3) idempotent add, (4) invalid-sig rejection preserves state, (5) commutative add ordering, (6) full().is_member iff valid_signal. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/signal/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:24:38.440961830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:26:14.520499037Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:26:14.518577480Z","close_reason":"Added 7 SigSet MR proptests in commit 38830fa covering algebraic properties: invertive add/del, idempotent add, commutative order, invalid-sig state preservation, full()⇔valid_signal consistency, bulk add+del roundtrip, absorbing del-absent. ~1790 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-10pq","title":"Cross-cutting: Architecture heterogeneity (aarch64/x86_64/RISC-V)","description":"Multi-ISA support: 31 ISA targets with different atomic/futex semantics. Priority: x86_64 (primary), aarch64 (active bring-up), RISC-V (future). Must handle: different atomic ordering guarantees, futex syscall variations, SIMD multiarch dispatch (IFUNC-equivalent). SOS barrier certificates must be ISA-independent.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.526480149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:24:33.147137017Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","critique","cross-platform","feature-parity","frankenlibc","gap-closure","pthread","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10pq","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10pq","depends_on_id":"bd-ldj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-11g","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Pattern extraction from FrankenSQLite spec (systematic+repair+proof+tuning)","description":"Extract and restate the RaptorQ design pattern from FrankenSQLite in a runtime_math-compatible form.

Key spec concepts to port (pattern-level):
- Systematic fast path: read/store source symbols without GF(256) solve.
- Deterministic repair symbol generation from (ObjectId/epoch seed, K, symbol_size).
- Two overheads: additive decode slack + multiplicative loss budget.
- Optional adaptive overhead tuning using anytime-valid evidence (e-process) + evidence ledger.
- Explainable decode proofs on failure.

Deliverable:
- A short, self-contained design note (in bead comment) that future work can implement without re-reading the FrankenSQLite spec.","design":"Design note captured in-repo: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/raptorq_pattern_frankensqlite.md (systematic+repair symbols, deterministic repair schedule, overhead formula, anytime-valid tuning hook, decode proofs; strict/hardened constraints).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.102392443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:01:16.006607654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:01:16.006484483Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":624,"issue_id":"bd-11g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Note: RaptorQ Pattern (FrankenSQLite) Restated for glibc_rust Runtime

### Why We Want This In libc
We already have runtime_math controllers that make membrane decisions (Allow / FullValidate / Repair / Deny). When something bad happens we want an *audit-grade* trail that survives partial loss (ring-buffer overwrites, sampling, crashes, dropped events) and remains explainable.

RaptorQ's key “alien artifact” idea is **appendable redundancy**:
- emit cheap *systematic* symbols first (fast path)
- optionally emit deterministic *repair* symbols later (background cadence)
- decode and generate proofs offline (harness/tooling), not on the hot path

### Constraints (glibc_rust-specific)
- No `/dp/asupersync` runtime dependency. Runtime may implement a tiny encoder itself.
- Strict mode must not pay codec costs on the hot path.
- Hardened mode may pay bounded overhead but must remain deterministic and measurable.

### Portable Pattern (What We Copy)
1. **Source block / epoching**
   - Group a bounded window of evidence events into an *epoch*.
   - Epoch defines: `K_source` (number of systematic records), `T` (record/symbol size), and seed.
2. **Systematic fast path**
   - Emit/store raw evidence records as systematic symbols `ESI = 0..K_source-1`.
   - Layout should favor sequential capture/export (contiguous run) to minimize read overhead in tooling.
3. **Deterministic repair generation**
   - Generate repair symbols `ESI = K_source..K_source+R-1` deterministically from `(epoch_id, K_source, T, policy)`.
   - Determinism is the superpower: tooling can reproduce encoding schedule and validate.
4. **Overhead = additive slack + multiplicative budget**
   - Keep an additive decode slack `slack_decode` (FrankenSQLite V1 uses `+2`).
   - Add multiplicative overhead `overhead_percent` as needed.
5. **Anytime-valid tuning**
   - Use an anytime-valid monitor (e-process) to decide when to raise redundancy.
   - Record changes in an evidence ledger.
6. **Decode proofs**
   - When tooling decodes/repairs, it emits a proof artifact describing which symbols were used and why the decode is trusted.

### Concrete Formulas (From FrankenSQLite Spec Excerpts)
These are the “load-bearing” knobs we can reuse verbatim:

- Additive slack:
  - `slack_decode = 2` (default)

- Repair symbol count selection:
  - `R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))`

- Back-of-envelope loss fraction tolerated (engineering approximation):
  - `loss_fraction_max ≈ max(0, (R - slack_decode) / (K_source + R))`

### Deterministic Seed Derivation (Portable Rule)
FrankenSQLite uses a deterministic seed derived from a stable identifier (e.g., changeset id):
- `seed = xxh3_64(id_bytes)`

glibc_rust mapping recommendation:
- Define an `epoch_id` that is stable within-process (and includes a monotonic counter).
- Derive seed from `(epoch_id || build_id || mode)` to avoid collisions across runs.

### Record/Envelope Mapping (What We Emit)
FrankenSQLite’s SymbolRecord idea maps cleanly to libc evidence:

- **EvidenceSymbolRecord** (proposal)
  - Header: `{ magic, version, epoch_id, seqno, family, mode, action, flags }`
  - Coding: `{ esi, k_source, r_repair, symbol_size_T, seed }`
  - Integrity: `{ payload_hash (xxh3_128 or blake3), prev_hash (hash-chain) }`
  - Payload: fixed-size `T` bytes (small; e.g., 64–256B), or padded.

Notes:
- “Auth tags” are optional: for libc evidence we may just do hash-chains + optional MAC in tooling.
- Source symbols should be cheap to write (memcpy into ring buffer + atomic seqno).
- Repair symbols should be generated on cadence (every N decisions, timer tick, or on exit).

### Where Decode Happens
- Runtime: encode only (systematic always; repair optional).
- Harness/tooling: decode, verify hashes/chain, emit DecodeProof.

### Open Design Choice (Explicit)
We must choose whether runtime implements:
- a small XOR-only fountain (LT-like) code (fastest, simplest), or
- a tighter RaptorQ-compatible schedule (harder), or
- “RaptorQ-inspired” parity that tooling decodes with a matching decoder.

This is decided in `bd-3a9`.

### Acceptance Criteria For The Pattern Extraction
- A concrete mapping of the above into glibc_rust terms (epoch, record format, seed, R selection).
- Explicit non-goals: no GF(256) Gaussian solve on hot path; no asupersync runtime dep.
- A minimal test matrix for future implementation:
  - drop/bitflip simulation
  - deterministic reproduction
  - decode-proof verification","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:50:28Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-luc3d","title":"printf_conformance executor ignores length modifiers (%Lf/%td/%zd/%jd); causes varargs width mismatch","description":"crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs run_host_snprintf takes PrintfArg::Int and passes c_int to libc::snprintf, but when the format specifier uses length modifiers %td (ptrdiff_t), %zd (size_t), %jd (intmax_t), %ld, %lld, glibc reads the arg at the wider width and gets garbage upper bits. Fix: when parsing args, inspect the format string for length modifiers and promote numeric args to PrintfArg::Long before dispatch. Surfaced by sprintf_t_d in bd-12hh matrix (expected '-12345', host returned '4294954951' due to stack-read overflow).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:32:32.945225484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:38:59.829032838Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-luc3d","title":"printf_conformance executor ignores length modifiers (%Lf/%td/%zd/%jd); causes varargs width mismatch","description":"crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs run_host_snprintf takes PrintfArg::Int and passes c_int to libc::snprintf, but when the format specifier uses length modifiers %td (ptrdiff_t), %zd (size_t), %jd (intmax_t), %ld, %lld, glibc reads the arg at the wider width and gets garbage upper bits. Fix: when parsing args, inspect the format string for length modifiers and promote numeric args to PrintfArg::Long before dispatch. Surfaced by sprintf_t_d in bd-12hh matrix (expected '-12345', host returned '4294954951' due to stack-read overflow).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:32:32.945225484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:02:07.937724297Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-lxm5","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_shadow differential conformance harness","description":"Integrate with franken_shadow for differential conformance: run FrankenLibC vs glibc/musl and compare outputs. Must support the suite-wide shadow-run as DEFAULT adoption wedge with hierarchical delta debugging for auto-minimizing failing traces. Reuse shared harness, do not fork.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.110477928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T22:51:13.437491264Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T22:51:13.437237333Z","close_reason":"Differential conformance: scripts/check_differential_glibc.sh (100% parity on 44 test cases). franken_shadow approach implemented via LD_PRELOAD comparison.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","conformance","cross-crate","frankenlibc","shadow"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxm5","depends_on_id":"bd-2hh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lxm5","depends_on_id":"bd-epeg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lyzd","title":"fts: implement children/list-control parity and ABI tests","description":"Close the exported FTS control-surface gap in unistd_abi. Scope: validate fts_open option/path errors; implement fts_children linked-list semantics for root/current directory cases including FTS_NAMEONLY and errno behavior; implement fts_set instruction handling for 0/FTS_AGAIN/FTS_FOLLOW/FTS_SKIP plus EINVAL on invalid instr; add ABI integration tests against host libc semantics for a tempdir traversal tree.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:30:28.712587043Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T16:53:19.238134851Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T16:53:19.236171163Z","close_reason":"Implemented FTS traversal-control parity and host-ABI tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fts","parity","unistd"]}
 {"id":"bd-m0ku","title":"syscall: signalfd4 NULL mask should keep EFAULT even with invalid flags","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for signalfd4(-1, NULL, 8, -1). Add ABI regression coverage proving the null-mask fault dominates bad flags when sizemask is valid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.593609083Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:45:35.671702674Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:45:35.669548289Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -267,20 +267,11 @@ fn printf_conformance_covers_special_values() {
 /// rather than failing so dispatch/packaging coverage stays green; each
 /// underlying gap is tracked in its own bead.
 const KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
-    // bd-luc3d: executor passes c_int/c_double without honoring length
-    // modifiers (%td needs ptrdiff_t/i64, %Lf needs long double/f80);
-    // glibc reads the wider width from varargs and host_parity
-    // collapses. Needs format-aware arg-width promotion.
-    ("sprintf_t_d", "bd-luc3d"),
+    // bd-luc3d: long double (%Lf/%Le) is 80-bit on x86_64 and has no
+    // Rust representation, so we cannot preserve it across the
+    // varargs boundary from a JSON f64 fixture value.
     ("sprintf_Lf_basic", "bd-luc3d"),
     ("sprintf_Le_basic", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_ll_d", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_j_d", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_llx_max", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_lld_negative", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_zd_negative", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_l_d", "bd-luc3d"),
-    ("sprintf_lo_max", "bd-luc3d"),
     // bd-qij6l: modern glibc disables %n in snprintf (FORTIFY); our
     // impl honors it. Parity check needs either fixture relaxation or
     // a host-side wrapper. sprintf_n_with_args also triggers a
